name: Achieve Beyond Pediatric Therapy & Autism Services
address: 7000 Austin St, Suite 200, Forest Hills, NY 11375
description: Achieve Beyond provides ABA therapy and related pediatric services for children diagnosed with Autism Spectrum Disorder across New York and New Jersey. Services are delivered in clinic, home, and community settings and are covered by health insurance.

name: Achieve Beyond – Westfield NJ
description: Achieve Beyond's Westfield, NJ clinic offers ABA therapy and pediatric services for children on the autism spectrum. Insurance is accepted and services are available in clinic and home settings.

name: AST Autism Spectrum Therapies – Orange CA
address: 625 The City Dr, Ste 120, Orange, CA 92868
description: Autism Spectrum Therapies Orange County clinic provides individualized ABA therapy for children with autism. The center offers both center-based and in-home programs for children from toddlers through school age.

name: Cleveland Clinic Children's Center for Autism
address: 2801 Martin Luther King Jr. Dr., Cleveland, OH 44104
description: Cleveland Clinic Children's Center for Autism provides comprehensive evaluation, treatment, education, and research services for children, adolescents, and young adults with autism spectrum disorders. The center includes the Lerner School for Autism, a certified non-public day school serving students through age 21.

name: University of Rochester Regional Center for Autism Spectrum Disorders
address: 200 East River Road, 3rd Floor, Rochester, NY 14623
description: The Rochester Regional Center for Autism Spectrum Disorders at Golisano Children's Hospital provides diagnostic evaluations, evidence-based behavioral treatment, and caregiver support for individuals across the autism spectrum. The center also engages in clinical research and professional training.

name: Tufts Floating Hospital – Center for Children with Special Needs (Autism)
description: The Center for Children with Special Needs at Floating Hospital for Children at Tufts Medical Center offers comprehensive evaluation, diagnosis, advocacy, and support for children with autism spectrum disorder, serving as an entry point to a wide range of treatment options in the Greater Boston area.

name: Jefferson Center for Autism and Neurodiversity (JeffCAN)
description: Jefferson Health's Center for Autism and Neurodiversity (JeffCAN) serves the Greater Philadelphia region with comprehensive assessment, diagnosis, and personalized care plans that integrate medical, educational, and therapeutic services. The center is designed with neurodiverse patients in mind.

name: Nemours Swank Autism Center – Delaware Valley
description: The Swank Autism Center at Nemours Children's Health provides comprehensive autism care including diagnosis, therapy, and family support in the Delaware Valley region. The center offers an integrated, multidisciplinary approach for children and adolescents with autism spectrum disorder.

name: Phoenix Children's Hospital Autism Program
description: Phoenix Children's Hospital Autism Program provides a comprehensive, patient- and family-centered approach to evaluating and caring for children with autism or those who may be on the autism spectrum. The program spans developmental pediatrics, psychology, and behavioral health.

name: Cook Children's Child Study Center – Autism
description: Cook Children's Child Study Center provides autism evaluations, ABA therapy, and behavioral consultation for children with developmental disabilities in the Fort Worth area. The team uses evidence-based assessment and treatment programs for children diagnosed with autism spectrum disorder.

name: Dell Children's Medical Center – Texas Child Study Center (Autism)
description: The Texas Child Study Center at Dell Children's Medical Center diagnoses, treats, and manages care for children with autism spectrum disorders and other developmental disabilities. The program uses an evidence-based multidisciplinary assessment and treatment model in Austin, Texas.

name: UnityPoint Blank Children's – Developmental Center (Autism)
description: The Developmental Center at Blank Children's Hospital provides evaluation, diagnosis, and treatment referrals for children with autism spectrum disorder and other developmental disabilities. The psychology clinic offers behavioral consultation for children ages 2-16 with ASD.

name: Atrium Health Wake Forest Baptist – Brenner Children's Developmental Pediatrics
description: Brenner Children's at Atrium Health Wake Forest Baptist provides comprehensive autism evaluations and behavioral pediatric services for children in North Carolina. The multidisciplinary team offers diagnosis, behavioral intervention, and family support through developmental and behavioral pediatrics.

name: Bittersweet Farms – Adult Autism Residential & Day Programs
description: Bittersweet Farms provides residential, vocational, educational, and recreational services for adults with autism across three farmstead locations in Northwest Ohio. The organization uses agricultural and nature-based activities to promote daily living skills, independence, and well-being.

name: Devereux Advanced Behavioral Health – Pennsylvania
description: Devereux Advanced Behavioral Health Pennsylvania provides specialized residential, educational, and behavioral health services for children and adults with autism and intellectual disabilities. Devereux is a national nonprofit operating across 13 states with over a century of service.

name: Devereux Advanced Behavioral Health – New Jersey
description: Devereux Advanced Behavioral Health New Jersey offers residential and educational services for individuals with autism, intellectual disabilities, and related behavioral health needs. The NJ programs include day school and residential options for children and adults.

name: Melmark – Autism & Developmental Disabilities Services
description: Melmark is a nonprofit multi-state human services provider offering clinically sophisticated, evidence-based special education, residential, vocational, and therapeutic services for children and adults with autism spectrum disorders and intellectual disabilities. Their Pennsylvania campus spans over 80 acres.

name: New Story Schools – Autism & Special Education
description: New Story Schools operates private special education day schools in Pennsylvania, Ohio, Virginia, North Carolina, Maryland, New Jersey, and New York. Schools serve students with autism spectrum disorder and behavioral and emotional challenges, with the goal of returning students to their home school districts.

name: The Treatment and Learning Centers (TLC) – Maryland
description: TLC - The Treatment and Learning Centers is a private nonprofit founded in 1950 in Rockville, MD, providing educational, therapeutic, and employment services for individuals with autism, language, and learning differences. Programs include a private day school (K-12), outpatient therapy, and vocational transition services.

name: Spectrum Works – Autism Employment NJ
description: Spectrum Works is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit dedicated to providing job training and employment opportunities for autistic high school students and young adults. The organization builds inclusive workforces at partner companies and provides coaching and support for employees on the autism spectrum.

name: College Internship Program (CIP) – Autism Transition
description: The College Internship Program (CIP) is a full-year private young adult transition program for individuals 18-26 with autism, ADHD, and other learning differences, offering comprehensive support in college, employment, and independent living. Centers are located across the US including Massachusetts, California, Indiana, Colorado, and Florida.

name: Easterseals Serving Chicagoland and Greater Rockford – Autism
description: Easterseals Serving Chicagoland and Greater Rockford operates three Autism Therapeutic Schools and a comprehensive array of programs providing education, evidence-based therapeutic interventions, and special assistance to individuals and families living with autism in the Chicago metro area.

name: Easterseals Greater Houston – Autism Services
description: Easterseals Greater Houston provides a wide variety of services for individuals with autism spectrum disorder, helping children and young adults learn problem-solving, make social adjustments, build responsibility, and gain new skills to increase self-esteem. Insurance accepted.

name: Easterseals Eastern Pennsylvania – Autism Services
address: 3111 W. Allegheny Ave, Suite 100, Philadelphia, PA 19132
description: Easterseals Eastern Pennsylvania provides early, individualized autism support through therapy, education, and family services at multiple Philadelphia-area locations. Programs include autism diagnostic clinics, ABA therapy, speech and occupational therapy for children with autism spectrum disorder.

name: Easterseals UCP North Carolina – Charlotte Autism Center
description: Easterseals UCP North Carolina and Virginia's Charlotte Autism Center provides ABA therapy and autism support services for children and families in the Charlotte metro area. The organization serves multiple NC counties including Durham, Johnston, New Hanover, Pender, and Wake.

name: The Color of Autism Foundation
description: The Color of Autism Foundation is a national nonprofit dedicated to supporting African American children with autism and their families by providing culturally competent resources for early and accurate diagnosis and empowering families to advocate for services. Programs include the LEEP! peer support group for Black fathers.

name: Autism in Black Inc.
description: Autism in Black Inc. is a neurodivergent-led 501(c)(3) nonprofit founded by Maria Davis-Pierre that provides education, advocacy, and community support to Black parents raising children on the autism spectrum. The organization works to reduce stigma and increase access to services in the Black community.

name: Black Autism Coalition (BAC)
description: The Black Autism Coalition promotes a culture of acceptance, understanding, and empowerment for autistic individuals and families in the Black and brown community. The national nonprofit works to address racial disparities in autism diagnosis and access to services.

name: Black Autism Support Society (BASS)
description: The Black Autism Support Society (BASS) is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit addressing support gaps and challenging the narrative around autism in the Black community through four pillars: Social Justice, Individual/Family, Education, and Spirituality. The organization provides autism resource support for individuals, parents, siblings, and caregivers.

name: Autism Faith Network
description: The Autism Faith Network works to spread autism awareness, acceptance, and inclusion in faith-based communities worldwide. Since 2016 the organization has partnered with over 102 churches, offering training, sensory tools, and resources to help congregations welcome and include autistic individuals.

name: Job Accommodation Network (JAN) – Autism Employment
description: The Job Accommodation Network (JAN) is a free consulting service funded by the US Department of Labor providing information about job accommodations for employees with disabilities, including autism. JAN offers individualized guidance on workplace supports for autistic employees and accommodations for job candidates.

name: Beyond BookSmart – Executive Function Coaching
address: 460 Hillside Avenue, Suite C, Needham, MA 02494
description: Beyond BookSmart provides 1:1 executive function coaching for students and adults with autism, ADHD, and learning differences, offering online sessions nationally. Coaches support clients in organization, time management, academic success, and life management skills from elementary school through adulthood.

name: Forta Health – Virtual ABA Therapy
description: Forta Health is the nation's largest Virtual ABA Network, providing online ABA therapy for children with autism through board-certified BCBAs in 43 states. Forta's family-powered model trains parents as active partners in therapy, with no waitlists and most families starting within 90 days.

name: Catalight Foundation – Autism Care Services
address: 2820 Shadelands Drive, Suite 200, Walnut Creek, CA 94598
description: Catalight Foundation provides value-based behavioral health services for individuals with autism and intellectual/developmental disabilities, available in person and via telehealth. Catalight's FDA-authorized Canvas Dx diagnostic aid and the RUBI parent training program support earlier and more accessible autism diagnosis and care.

name: Above & Beyond ABA Therapy
description: Above and Beyond ABA Therapy provides quality Applied Behavior Analysis services for children ages 1-18 diagnosed with autism in New Jersey, Virginia, Georgia, Colorado, and North Carolina. The organization was founded in 2020 and offers in-home, school-based, and center-based therapy options.

name: Therapy and Beyond – ABA & Autism Therapy (TX/OK/CO)
description: Therapy and Beyond provides individualized ABA, speech, and occupational therapy for individuals with autism and developmental disabilities across Texas, Oklahoma, and Colorado. The organization is owned and operated by a BCBA-D with multiple clinic locations.

name: Ascend Autism – ABA Therapy Centers
address: 22 Saw Mill River Rd, Hawthorne, NY 10532
description: Ascend Autism provides early intervention and targeted ABA services at centers across New York, Connecticut, Pennsylvania, and Tennessee for children diagnosed with autism spectrum disorder. The organization offers center-based treatment with a focus on individualized, evidence-based programs.

name: Behavior Therapy Associates – NJ Autism Services
description: Behavior Therapy Associates provides comprehensive psychological, behavioral, and educational services for individuals with autism and developmental disabilities in New Jersey and Pennsylvania, and via telehealth across many PSYPACT states. Serving NJ families since 1979 with evidence-based practices.

name: Bluebell ABA – Georgia
description: Bluebell ABA provides personalized in-home ABA therapy for children with autism across Georgia, including Sandy Springs, Brookhaven, and surrounding communities. The organization creates comfortable, familiar home environments for therapy and accepts major insurance providers.

name: Building Bridges Behavior Therapy – NJ
description: Building Bridges Behavior Therapy in Neptune, New Jersey provides ABA therapy and behavioral support services for children and adults with autism spectrum disorder. The organization offers both in-home and center-based services in Monmouth County and surrounding areas.

name: Cardinal Pediatric Therapies – ABA Autism
address: 1415 W NC 54 Hwy, Suite 101, Durham, NC 27707
description: Cardinal Pediatric Therapies provides high-quality, evidence-based ABA therapy for children with autism, with locations in North Carolina and Arizona (including a Phoenix North Mountain Center opened in 2025). The organization offers in-clinic therapy, parent training, social skills groups, and classroom readiness programs.

name: Autism Village – Community App & Resource Directory
description: Autism Village is a crowdsourced mobile app and online platform designed to help autism families locate autism-friendly places, services, and resources by location. Users can search, review, and share information about autism-friendly businesses, therapists, and community resources nationwide.

name: Mayo Clinic – Autism Spectrum Disorder Services
description: Mayo Clinic in Rochester, Minnesota provides comprehensive autism spectrum disorder evaluations and care through its multidisciplinary team of neurodevelopmental pediatricians, psychologists, and behavioral health specialists. Mayo Clinic is a world-renowned academic medical center serving patients nationally and internationally.

name: Sonoran Center for Excellence in Disabilities – UA Autism Programs
description: The Sonoran Center for Excellence in Disabilities at the University of Arizona is a federally designated University Center for Excellence in Developmental Disabilities (UCEDD), conducting autism research, professional training through the LEND program, and community outreach for individuals and families across Arizona.

name: Waisman Center UCEDD – UW-Madison Autism Programs
description: The Waisman Center UCEDD at the University of Wisconsin-Madison is a federally designated University Center for Excellence in Developmental Disabilities providing research, training, technical assistance, and information dissemination related to autism and other developmental disabilities.

name: USC UCEDD – Developmental Disabilities Center at CHLA
description: The USC University Center for Excellence in Developmental Disabilities (UCEDD) at Children's Hospital Los Angeles conducts autism research, professional training through the LEND program, and community services for individuals with autism and developmental disabilities in Southern California.

name: Thompson Center for Autism – University of Missouri
description: The Thompson Center for Autism and Neurodevelopmental Disorders at the University of Missouri provides comprehensive evaluations, ABA therapy, behavioral health services, and autism research for children and adults. The Thompson Center is Missouri's only comprehensive autism treatment and research center affiliated with a major research university.

name: Phoenix Autism Center – ABA Therapy
address: 4801 E McDowell Rd, Suite 175, Phoenix, AZ 85008
description: Phoenix Autism Center is a family-owned ABA therapy center providing center-based Applied Behavior Analysis and speech and language therapy for children with autism in the Phoenix metro area. The center is known for personalized, high-quality care in a supportive environment.

name: Goodwill NY/NJ – Autism & Developmental Disabilities Employment
description: Goodwill NY/NJ provides employment, vocational training, and day services for adults with autism and developmental disabilities through its Habilitation and Employment Services facility in Astoria, New York. Programs include day habilitation, supported employment, and job coaching aligned to individual goals.

name: Autism and Faith – Neurodivergent Faith Community
description: Autism and Faith is a nonprofit organization dedicated to bridging the gap between faith communities and individuals with autism and other neurodivergent needs. The organization trains and resources places of worship to create welcoming, inclusive environments for autistic individuals and their families.

name: Autism Behavioral Health (ABH)
description: Autism Behavioral Health (ABH) provides scientifically validated Applied Behavior Analysis treatment to enhance the lives of individuals with autism spectrum disorder and related disorders. ABH's partnership model brings together families, providers, and referring agencies to deliver individualized, goal-based ABA therapy.

name: Amaze ABA – Georgia
description: Amaze ABA provides in-home and center-based ABA therapy for children with autism in Georgia. The organization focuses on individualized, naturalistic therapy programs for young children and school-age individuals on the autism spectrum, with BCBA supervision.

name: Autism NOW Center – National Autism Resource & Information
address: 1825 K Street NW, Suite 1200, Washington, DC 20006
description: The Autism NOW Center is a national information and referral resource for individuals with autism spectrum disorders and other developmental disabilities, their families, and professionals. Operated by The Arc of the United States, Autism NOW provides webinars, guides, and person-centered planning resources.

name: SPARK for Autism – Research Registry
description: SPARK (Simons Foundation Powering Autism Research for Knowledge) is the largest autism research study in the US, seeking to enroll 50,000 individuals with autism and their family members. Participants contribute to genetic and behavioral research and gain access to autism resources and community.

name: PEAK Parent Center – Colorado PTI (Autism)
address: 611 North Weber Street, Suite 200, Colorado Springs, CO 80903
description: PEAK Parent Center is Colorado's federally funded Parent Training and Information (PTI) Center providing free training, information, and advocacy to families of children with disabilities including autism. PEAK helps parents understand special education rights and partner effectively with schools.

name: PAVE – Partnerships for Action Voices for Empowerment (WA PTI)
description: PAVE (Partnerships for Action Voices for Empowerment) is Washington State's federally funded Parent Training and Information Center, providing free training and information to families of children with disabilities including autism. PAVE helps parents navigate special education, IEPs, and disability services throughout Washington.

name: Partners Resource Network – Texas PTI (Autism)
address: 1090 Longfellow Drive, Suite B, Beaumont, TX 77706
description: Partners Resource Network is Texas's federally funded Parent Training and Information (PTI) Center providing training, information, and support to families of children with disabilities including autism. Partners helps Texas families understand special education rights and participate effectively in their child's educational planning.

name: Matrix Parent Network – California PTI Bay Area
address: 94 Galli Drive, Suite C, Novato, CA 94949
description: Matrix Parent Network is a federally funded Parent Training and Information (PTI) Center serving families of children with disabilities including autism in California. Matrix provides free advocacy training, IEP support, and bilingual resources for families navigating special education in the San Francisco Bay Area.

name: Empower BH – ABA Therapy Houston TX
description: Empower BH provides ABA therapy for children with autism in Texas, with clinic and in-home services across the Houston area. The organization offers individualized ABA programs supervised by BCBAs and accepts most major insurance plans.

name: Texas ABA Centers
description: Texas ABA Centers provides ABA therapy and autism diagnostic services across Texas with multiple locations. The organization specializes in early intervention for young children with autism and offers center-based services without long waitlists.

name: American Autism & Rehabilitation Center (AARC)
description: The American Autism and Rehabilitation Center provides ABA therapy, speech therapy, and occupational therapy for children and adolescents with autism in Maryland. AARC's evidence-based, multidisciplinary approach supports individualized goals for each child's communication, social, and behavioral development.

name: Southern Oregon ABA – Autism Therapy
address: 1123 SW Umatilla Ave, Grants Pass, OR 97526
description: Southern Oregon ABA provides ABA therapy for children with autism in the Grants Pass, Medford, and surrounding Southern Oregon region. The organization offers in-home and clinic-based behavioral services in a largely rural area, with BCBA supervision and individualized programming.

name: Milestones Autism Resources – Cleveland
description: Milestones Autism Resources is a Cleveland-based nonprofit providing community resource directories, education programs, and professional training to support individuals with autism and their families in Northeast Ohio. The organization hosts an annual autism conference and maintains an extensive online resource center.

name: Rocky Mountain Autism Center
address: 7200 S. Alton Way, Suite B-250, Centennial, CO 80112
description: Rocky Mountain Autism Center provides autism diagnostic evaluations, ABA therapy, social skills groups, and family support for children and adolescents in the Denver metro area. The center's multidisciplinary team offers comprehensive care from assessment through treatment.

name: Applied ABC – ABA Therapy Network
address: 18 Exchange Place, Suite 300, Jersey City, NJ 07302
description: Applied ABC is an ABA therapy provider operating across multiple states including New Jersey, New York, Massachusetts, Georgia, Texas, and other states. The organization provides in-home, center-based, and school-based ABA services for children with autism, with BCBA-supervised treatment teams.

name: Early Autism Services – Pacific Northwest
address: 12360 SW Canyon Rd, Suite 100, Beaverton, OR 97005
description: Early Autism Services provides early intensive ABA therapy for young children with autism in Oregon and Washington. The organization focuses on naturalistic, play-based intervention and offers center-based programs with high-quality BCBA supervision.

name: Autism Empowerment – Pacific Northwest
description: Autism Empowerment is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit in Southwest Washington providing autism resource navigation, support groups, social events, and community programs built on principles of acceptance, enrichment, and empowerment. The organization serves individuals with autism and their families across the Vancouver-Portland metro region.

name: Children's Specialized Hospital ABA – New Jersey
description: Children's Specialized ABA is a network of ABA therapy clinics affiliated with Children's Specialized Hospital serving children with autism across New Jersey. Services include center-based and in-home ABA therapy with BCBA oversight and multiple clinic locations statewide.

name: Center for Social Dynamics (CSD) – Autism Services
address: 1535 Merced Street, Suite 100, San Leandro, CA 94577
description: Center for Social Dynamics (CSD) provides ABA therapy and related services for children with autism across California, Georgia, Texas, Virginia, and other states. CSD's evidence-based programs include center-based ABA, in-home services, speech therapy, and occupational therapy with individualized treatment planning.

name: Autism Services Inc. – Western New York
description: Autism Services Inc. has been providing comprehensive supports to individuals with autism in Western New York since 1971. The organization offers residential programs, day habilitation, supported employment, and educational services for children and adults with autism across the Buffalo-Niagara region.

name: Pathfinders for Autism – Maryland
description: Pathfinders for Autism is a Maryland-based nonprofit providing a comprehensive resource directory, helpline, and family support services for individuals with autism and their families. The organization maintains an extensive database of Maryland autism providers and offers personalized resource navigation.

name: Nationwide Children's Hospital – Autism Center
description: Nationwide Children's Hospital Autism Center in Columbus, Ohio provides comprehensive autism evaluations, ABA therapy, behavioral health services, and cutting-edge research for children and adolescents with autism spectrum disorder. The center's interdisciplinary team offers individualized, family-centered care.

name: Marcus Autism Center – Children's Healthcare of Atlanta
description: Marcus Autism Center, a partnership of Children's Healthcare of Atlanta and Emory University, is one of the nation's largest autism centers providing diagnostic evaluations, intensive behavioral treatment, and clinical research. The center treats over 9,000 patients annually and offers the unique Severe Behavior Day Treatment program.

name: UC Davis MIND Institute – Autism Research & Treatment
description: The UC Davis MIND Institute is an internationally recognized research and treatment center dedicated to understanding autism and other neurodevelopmental disorders. The Institute offers comprehensive diagnostic evaluations, clinical trials, evidence-based treatment, and training for future autism specialists in California.

name: Fraser – Autism Services Minnesota
description: Fraser is one of Minnesota's largest disability service providers, offering ABA therapy, mental health services, employment support, housing, and early childhood programs for individuals with autism across the lifespan. Fraser's comprehensive continuum of care serves thousands of Minnesotans with autism annually.

name: Institute for Applied Behavior Analysis (IABA)
address: 5901 Green Valley Circle, Suite 100, Culver City, CA 90230
description: The Institute for Applied Behavior Analysis (IABA) is a California-based nonprofit providing ABA therapy, intensive behavioral intervention, and residential services for individuals with autism, intellectual disabilities, and challenging behaviors. IABA serves both children and adults and trains clinicians nationally.

name: Autism Learning Partners – Multi-State ABA
address: 11622 El Camino Real, Suite 100, San Diego, CA 92130
description: Autism Learning Partners is a leading ABA therapy provider serving children with autism across California, Massachusetts, Oregon, Washington, Arizona, and several other states. The organization provides center-based and in-home ABA therapy with a focus on naturalistic, play-based, and individualized approaches.

name: Rehabilitation Services Administration – Vocational Rehabilitation for Autism
description: The Rehabilitation Services Administration (RSA) oversees federally funded Vocational Rehabilitation (VR) programs in all 50 states serving individuals with disabilities including autism. State VR programs provide employment assessment, training, job placement, and supported employment to help autistic individuals achieve competitive integrated employment.

name: Autistic Women & Nonbinary Network (AWN)
description: The Autistic Women and Nonbinary Network (AWN) is a nonprofit advocacy organization providing community, support, and resources for autistic women, girls, and nonbinary individuals. AWN centers the voices of multiply-marginalized autistic people and works to address the unique challenges faced by women and nonbinary autistics.

name: Birth to Three System – Connecticut Autism Early Intervention
description: Connecticut's Birth to Three System provides early intervention services including autism screening, developmental therapy, and family support for children birth through age 3. The program offers individualized, family-centered services in natural environments for young children with or at risk for autism.

name: Autism Society of Central Ohio
address: One Easton Oval, Suite 120, Columbus, OH 43219
description: The Autism Society of Central Ohio is an Autism Society of America affiliate chapter providing resource navigation, family support, advocacy, and community programs for individuals with autism and their families in the Columbus metro area and surrounding Central Ohio counties.

name: Autism Society of the Ozarks
address: 2875 N Treehouse Ln, Suite 2, Fayetteville, AR 72703
description: The Autism Society of the Ozarks is an Autism Society of America affiliate chapter providing resource navigation, family support, and community education for individuals with autism and their families in Northwest Arkansas and the Ozarks region.

name: Autism Center of Pittsburgh
description: The Autism Center of Pittsburgh provides autism diagnostic evaluations, ABA therapy, social skills training, and family support for children and adolescents with autism in the Pittsburgh metro area. The center combines evidence-based intervention with family-centered care.

name: SPARK for Autism – Simons Foundation Research
description: SPARK (Simons Foundation Powering Autism Research for Knowledge) is the largest autism research study in the US, seeking to enroll 50,000 individuals with autism and their family members. Participants contribute to genetic and behavioral research and gain access to autism resources and community.

name: CDC Learn the Signs Act Early – Autism Awareness
services: Learn the Signs Act Early Campaign
description: The CDC's Autism and Developmental Disabilities Monitoring (ADDM) Network tracks autism prevalence and publishes free educational materials for families and clinicians. The CDC's Learn the Signs Act Early campaign provides developmental milestone resources to support early autism identification nationwide.

name: Autism NOW – National Information & Referral Center
address: 1825 K Street NW, Suite 1200, Washington, DC 20006
description: The Autism NOW Center is a national information and referral resource for individuals with autism spectrum disorders and other developmental disabilities, their families, and professionals. Operated by The Arc of the United States, Autism NOW provides webinars, guides, and person-centered planning resources.

name: Autism Society of West Virginia
description: The Autism Society of West Virginia is an Autism Society of America affiliate chapter serving families of individuals with autism throughout West Virginia with resource navigation, family support, and advocacy. The organization works to improve access to autism services across the state.

name: Autism Society of Greater Harrisburg
description: The Autism Society of Greater Harrisburg provides resource navigation, family support, adult day programs, and social events for individuals with autism in Central Pennsylvania. The organization is an Autism Society of America affiliate serving families across the greater Harrisburg region.

name: Autism Society of the Pittsburgh Area
description: The Autism Society of the Pittsburgh Area is an Autism Society of America affiliate chapter providing resource navigation, support groups, advocacy, and community programs for individuals with autism and their families in the Pittsburgh region.

name: Virginia Institute of Autism (VIA)
description: The Virginia Institute of Autism (VIA) is a nonprofit organization operating a day school for students with autism, providing ABA-based education and therapy for children K-12, as well as vocational and adult services for young adults transitioning beyond school age in Charlottesville, Virginia.

name: Vanderbilt Kennedy Center for Excellence in Developmental Disabilities
description: The Vanderbilt Kennedy Center at Vanderbilt University is a federally designated University Center for Excellence in Developmental Disabilities (UCEDD) conducting autism research, professional training through its LEND program, and community services for individuals with autism and other developmental disabilities in Tennessee.

name: Easter Seals Western and Central Pennsylvania
description: Easterseals Western and Central Pennsylvania operates programs in 47 counties through 5 office locations, providing autism support, ABA therapy, employment services, and family support to thousands of Pennsylvanians with disabilities. Services span Western, Central, and Southwestern Pennsylvania.

name: Autism Society of Delaware
address: 924 Old Harmony Rd, Suite 201, Newark, DE 19713
description: The Autism Society of Delaware is an Autism Society of America affiliate chapter providing resource navigation, family support, advocacy, and community programs for individuals with autism and their families across the state of Delaware.

name: Autism Society of Rhode Island
description: The Autism Society of Rhode Island is an Autism Society of America affiliate chapter providing resource navigation, family support, advocacy, and community programs for individuals with autism and their families across Rhode Island.

name: Autism Resource Center of Central Massachusetts
description: The Autism Resource Center of Central Massachusetts provides resource navigation, family support, advocacy, and community programs for individuals with autism and their families in the Worcester area and surrounding Central Massachusetts region.

name: Autism Society of Inland Empire – California
description: The Autism Society of the Inland Empire is an Autism Society of America affiliate chapter serving the Inland Empire region of California with resource navigation, family support, and advocacy for individuals with autism and their families.

name: Autism Society of Central Virginia
description: The Autism Society of Central Virginia is an Autism Society of America affiliate chapter providing resource navigation, family support, advocacy, and community programs for individuals with autism and their families in the Richmond metro area and Central Virginia.

name: Dan Marino Foundation – Autism Services FL
address: 2600 N Military Trail, Suite 470, Boca Raton, FL 33431
description: The Dan Marino Foundation provides autism-related research funding, family support, and community programs in Florida. The foundation is committed to improving the quality of life for individuals with autism spectrum disorder across the lifespan, with a focus on life skills and community inclusion.

name: Autism Society of Minnesota – Adult Services
address: 2380 Wycliff St, Suite 102, St. Paul, MN 55114
description: AuSM's Adult Services division provides recreational programs, social events, employment navigation, and housing resources specifically for adults with autism in Minnesota. Programming supports autistic adults in building community connections and living independently.

name: Easterseals Lonestar – North Texas Autism
address: 4443 N Josey Lane, Suite 106, Carrollton, TX 75010
description: Easterseals Lonestar provides autism ABA therapy, employment training, adult day services, and early childhood programs to individuals with disabilities in North Texas. The organization has served the Dallas-Fort Worth area since 1939 with a comprehensive range of disability services.
